account.title: Account details
account.intro: Your GreaseDesk account reference and status.

details.title: Company details
details.intro: Your business's registered details. These appear on quotes and invoices.
details.name: Company name
details.vatRegisteredHint: Off = no VAT on quotes, invoices or overheads (prices are just prices).
details.defaultVatRateHint: The company-wide default. Pre-fills new quotes and overheads; still editable per entry.

invoice.heading: Invoice numbering
invoice.nextNumberHint: Set your starting number before the first invoice is issued.
invoice.nextNumberLocked: Invoices have been issued — the sequence can no longer be changed (numbers must stay gapless).
invoice.warrantyPrefixHint: Warranty / comeback invoices (£0) use their own number series with this prefix.
invoice.emailFooterHint: A small footer line in invoice emails. Never printed on the invoice itself.
invoice.paidWindowHint: Marking an invoice paid holds it for this long before the customer's confirmation is sent — you can quietly unmark it in the window (e.g. a card payment that doesn't clear).
invoice.movedNote: Invoice numbering and sending options have moved to Settings → Invoicing.
invoice.formatLocked: Invoices have been issued, so the number format (prefix, padding, fiscal-year) is locked to protect the sequence. The clearance window stays editable.

invoicing.title: Invoicing
invoicing.intro: How your invoices look and how they reach customers. Numbering, sending, terms and logo live here.
invoicing.senderNameHint: Shown as the sender of invoice emails. The sending address stays GreaseDesk-owned for deliverability.
invoicing.replyToHint: Customer replies go here — your business address, not the login email.
invoicing.bccHint: Every invoice email is silently copied here so you keep a record.
invoicing.footerTextPh:
Payment due within 14 days.
We reserve the right to charge interest under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts Act.
Please quote your invoice number when paying.
invoicing.footerTextHint: Printed at the foot of every invoice, exactly as written.
invoicing.logoSaved: Logo saved — it now appears top-centre of the invoice PDF.
invoicing.logoHint: PNG or JPEG. Rendered top-centre of the PDF, auto-sized.
invoicing.pmIntro: How customers pay, and how each clears: instant (cash in the till), windowed (card — clears after the clearance window), or manual (warranty companies / EMAC — you confirm when the money lands).
invoicing.pmError: Could not save the payment method.
